t_ch: 密钥之一解锁，迷津隆隆作响，变换成新的通路；再让石球落入池中一次，即可打开暗门。
t_en: One of the encryption keys has been locked, and the confusion rumbles, becoming a new path. Let the stone ball fall into the pool once more, to open the hidden door.

t_ch: 迷津隆隆作响……
t_en: The Maze groans deeply...

t_ch: 请保持距离。
t_en: Please stay clear.

t_ch: 一段时间后 命运重渊
t_en: Some time later, in Abyss of Fate
Ugh...

t_ch: 这里是……
t_en: Where am I...

t_ch: 小灰，小白？还有……
t_en: Little Gray? Snowy? And...

t_ch: 等等…缇安呢？她回来了吗？
t_en: Wait... where's Trianne? Is she back?

t_ch: 我们赶到时，只看见两位倒在路边……
t_en: We only saw the two of you collapsed on the side of the road when we arrived...

t_ch: 我和丹宝也在附近搜索了一番…只是没能找到缇安大人的踪迹。
t_en: Dannie and I looked around, but we couldn't find any traces of Lady Trianne...

t_ch: 那个黑袍的剑士呢？
t_en: What about the black-robed swordmaster?

t_ch: …该死！果然是那家伙。
t_en: ...Blast it! Of course it's them!

t_ch: 我们还是来晚一步，{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的火种…恐怕已经遭遇不测。
t_en: We were still too late. I fear something bad has happened to {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx's{RUBY_E#} Coreflame.

t_ch: 那，缇安大人……
t_en: Then, Lady Trianne...

t_ch: 她的神力所剩无几。现在…感应微乎其微。
t_en: She doesn't have much divine power left. We can barely sense anything...

t_ch: 必须追击那黑袍剑士。
t_en: We need to go after that black-robed swordmaster.

t_ch: 缇安…有种不祥的预感。
t_en: Trianne... I have a bad feeling about this.

t_ch: 二位老师平安无事，已是不幸中的万幸。
t_en: That you're both safe is already a blessing.

t_ch: 各位，我们应当立即动身，兵分两路：一队留守重渊，和阿格莱雅的援兵会合，搜寻缇安老师的下落。
t_en: Everyone, we must take action now. We should split into two groups: One group will stay in the Abyss and meet up with Aglaea's reinforcements, then search for Trianne's whereabouts.

t_ch: 另一队随我出发，追缉那黑衣剑士，夺回火种。
t_en: The other group will come with me to pursue the black-robed swordmaster and recover the Coreflame.

t_ch: 你知道它的动向么？
t_en: Do you know where it's headed?

t_ch: 此前没来得及和二位说明，其实我和那刻夏老师对黑潮早有顾虑，已经有所准备。
t_en: I didn't get to tell you this before, but Anaxa and I already have our misgivings about the black tide, so we've made preparations.

t_ch: 详情可以路上再聊。战机转瞬即逝，必须抓紧时间。
t_en: I'll tell you the details as we move. Opportunities are fleeting on the battlefield. We should hurry.

t_ch: 报！白厄大人…啊，还有缇宝、缇宁大人！你们没事就好！
t_en: Lord Phainon! Ah... Lady Tribbie! And Lady Trinnon! I'm glad you're alright!

t_ch: 是阿格莱雅派人来了？
t_en: Did Aglaea's people get here?

t_ch: 正是！阿格莱雅大人已经知晓了情况，她还托人捎来口信——
t_en: Yes! Lady Aglaea already knows about the situation and sent along a message.

t_ch: 「白厄，我明白那剑士于你而言意义深重。但若无长策便急于冒进，那要为此白白送命的…恐怕远不止你一人了。」
t_en: "Phainon, I understand that swordmaster is of great importance to you, but if you charge in without a plan, I fear that... you won't be the only one who dies in vain."

t_ch: …当然。「仇恨是杀死英雄的毒酒」——我无意孤军奋战。若那剑士真与黑潮有关，绝不是黄金裔能轻视的对手。
t_en: ...I understand. "Hatred is a hero's deadliest poison." I have no intention of fighting alone. If that swordmaster really is related to the black tide, the Chrysos Heirs must not take them lightly.

t_ch: 我本就打算返回圣城，与阿格莱雅共议战略。这是一场围剿，我们需要更多人的帮助。
t_en: I have indeed planned to return to the holy city and discuss the strategy with Aglaea. We'll need all the help we can get for this encirclement.

t_ch: 不如按照先前分工，我和风堇留下来接应援兵。你带剩下的人返回圣城，告诉阿格莱雅你们的计划吧。
t_en: Why don't we split up as previously discussed? Hyacine and I will stay to regroup with the reinforcements. You go back to the holy city with everyone else and tell Aglaea about your plan.

t_ch: 缇宁也留在重渊。这样一旦有任何进展，两边可以有个照应。
t_en: Trinnon will stay in the Abyss, too. That way, both sides can communicate with each other if anything happens.

t_ch: 拜托各位了。{NICKNAME}，缇宝老师，我们走。
t_en: Thank you, everyone. {NICKNAME}, Tribbie, let's go.

t_ch: 异邦人的王也不过如此…
t_en: So much for the king of those outlanders...

t_ch: 奥赫玛的祸患…
t_en: A disaster for Okhema...

t_ch: 翁法罗斯没有你们的位置！
t_en: There's no place for you in Amphoreus!

t_ch: 你们要绝后了，异邦人！
t_en: This will be the end of you outlanders!

t_ch: 和死人作伴去吧！
t_en: Go find your friends in hell!

t_ch: 该死的野蛮人…
t_en: Cursed brutes...

t_ch: 嗯？那是知更鸟？她怎么来这儿了？
t_en: Hmm? Isn't that Robin? What's she doing here?

t_ch: 好久不见，{NICKNAME}。螺丝咕姆先生说你今天会来，我就在这儿等了一会儿。
t_en: It's been a while, {NICKNAME}. Mr. Screwllum said you'd stop by today, so I figured I'd wait here for a bit.

t_ch: 这台设备，就是测试「差分宇宙」的装置吗？
t_en: So this is the device for testing the "Divergent Universe," huh?

t_ch: 你也对差分宇宙感兴趣？
t_en: You're interested in the Divergent Universe too?

t_ch: 你误会了…虽然在折纸大学中选修过历史，不过我显然不是参与科研的合适人选。
t_en: Oh, you've got the wrong idea... I may have taken some history electives back at Paperfold University, but I'm hardly the right person for scientific research.

t_ch: 怎么不给我留个言呀？
t_en: Why didn't you leave me a message?

t_ch: 抱歉，突然出现在了这里。毕竟星穹列车的开拓之行总是来去匆匆的，我想尽量不干扰各位的行程。
t_en: Sorry for just showing up out of the blue. The Astral Express's trailblazing journey is always a whirlwind, so I try not to disrupt your plans.

t_ch: 我今天来是受人所托，为你带来一件礼物。
t_en: I'm here today on someone's behalf, to bring you a gift.

t_ch: 不知道你还记不记得，之前在匹诺康尼召开的那场「圣杯战争」？
t_en: Do you still remember the Holy Grail War we had back in Penacony?

t_ch: 完全不记得。
t_en: Not a clue.

t_ch: 哈哈，一点也不意外。这个叫做「圣杯」的奇物确实很古怪。
t_en: Haha, not surprising at all. This so-called "Holy Grail" Curio is bizarre indeed.

t_ch: 虽然还记得我们一起经历了这场冒险，但事后回想起来，有些细节却变得模糊起来了。
t_en: Even though I still recall that adventure we went through together, some of the details get a bit hazy when I think back on it.

t_ch: 当然记得啊！
t_en: You bet I do!

t_ch: 我就知道你不会忘记的。即便生活在匹诺康尼这样的梦中世界，这样的体验也很稀奇。
t_en: I knew you wouldn't forget. Even in a dreamland like Penacony, that was a once-in-a-lifetime experience.

t_ch: 不过，每次回想共同经历的冒险，有些细节总会变得模糊起来。这个叫做「圣杯」的奇物真是古怪。
t_en: However, every time I think back to our adventure, some details get a bit hazy. That so-called "Holy Grail" is such a bizarre Curio.

t_ch: 星穹列车这是开哪儿去了…？
t_en: Where did the Astral Express head to this time...?

t_ch: 圣…圣杯战争？！
t_en: The Ho... Holy Grail War!?

t_ch: 看起来，这个叫做「圣杯」的奇物确实很古怪。虽然还记得我们一起经历了这场冒险，但事后回想起来，有些细节却总会变得模糊起来。
t_en: It's indeed bizarre. This so-called "Holy Grail" Curio... Even though I still recall what we went through together, some of the details always get a bit hazy when I think back on it.

t_ch: 老奥帝先生将这樽「圣杯」赠给了我，作为对参与圣杯战争的人的补偿。我希望将它摆放在晖长石号上，他本人也表示赞同，按照他的说法……
t_en: Old Oti gave me this "Holy Grail" as a token of gratitude for everyone who participated in the war. I wanted to display it on the Radiant Feldspar. He agreed wholeheartedly, saying...

t_ch: 「嚯嗬嗬嗬，论妆点晖长石号的装饰品，还有什么能比号称可以实现一切愿望的杯子更加气派！」
t_en: "Hohohoho! What better decoration for the Radiant Feldspar than a cup said to grant any wish? Talk about grandeur!"

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆先生受托对「圣杯」进行了无害化的处理。现在的它不过是个寻常的梦泡，不会再有什么回应愿望的非凡特性了。
t_en: Mr. Screwllum was entrusted to neutralize the power of the "Holy Grail." Now it's just an ordinary dream bubble, stripped of its wish-granting properties.

t_ch: 我一直很好奇：如果当真能用这圣杯实现一个愿望，你想实现什么愿望？
t_en: I've always wondered: If the Grail really could grant you a wish, what would yours be?

t_ch: （想实现的愿望…似乎在进行「圣杯战争」的时候，也有人问过我类似的问题……）
t_en: (A wish, huh... Feels like someone asked me a similar question during the Holy Grail War...)

t_ch: 你的思绪渐渐回溯到了那场「圣杯战争」伊始，许多模糊的细节变得清晰了起来。
t_en: Your thoughts gradually rewind to the beginning of that Holy Grail War as many vague details gain clarity.

t_ch: 很久…或是不久之前。
t_en: It was a long time ago... or not so long ago...

t_ch: 嗯？那是知更鸟？她怎么来这儿了？
t_en: Hmm? Isn't that Robin? What's she doing here?

t_en: It's been a while, {NICKNAME}. Mr. Screwllum said you'd stop by today, so I figured I'd wait here for a bit.

t_ch: 你的思绪渐渐回溯到了那场「圣杯战争」伊始，许多模糊的细节变得清晰了起来。
t_en: Your thoughts gradually rewind to the beginning of that Holy Grail War as many vague details gain clarity.

t_ch: 很久…或是不久之前。
t_en: It was a long time ago... or not so long ago...

t_ch: 作为匹诺康尼的上一届盛会巨星，{NICKNAME}曾是万众瞩目的焦点。
t_en: As Penacony's last Festive Superstar, {NICKNAME} was once the center of everyone's attention.

t_ch: 而如今，史无前例的匹诺康尼第一届「圣杯战争」即将开幕，{NICKNAME}又再度回到了所有人的视线之中！
t_en: And now, with the unprecedented First Penaconian Holy Grail War about to kick off, {NICKNAME} is back in the spotlight!

t_ch: 来，{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}，向观众们展示一下你手上的那个…这叫啥来着…文身？
t_en: Come on, {M#buddy}{F#girl}, show the audience that... uh... what's it called... tattoo... on your hand?

t_ch: 是「令咒」！
t_en: It's the Command Spells!

t_ch: 没错！「令咒」！作为被选中参与「圣杯战争」的资格证明，听说凭它的力量，能号令匹诺康尼历史上的名人为你而战！
t_en: Right! Command Spells! Proof of your selection to join the Holy Grail War. They say its power lets you command legendary figures from Penacony's history to fight for you!

t_ch: 接下来，让我们将时间留给《水晶日报》的金牌记者～
t_en: And now, let's give up the floor to the star reporter of the Crystal Daily!

t_ch: 荣誉队员！如果成功获得「圣杯」，你想实现什么愿望呢？
t_en: So, honorary member! If you win the Holy Grail, what wish would you want granted?

t_ch: 有花不完的钱。
t_en: I want to swim in money!

t_ch: 我希望宇宙和平！
t_en: I wish for peace across the universe!

t_ch: 也该让我成为欢愉星神了吧？
t_en: Is it time for me to become the Aeon of Elation?

t_ch: 我的愿望是再多要两个愿望！
t_en: I'd wish for two more wishes!

t_ch: 你俩为什么出现在这里？
t_en: Why are you two even here?

t_ch: 喔！荣誉队员在回避本记者的提问哦，该不会是有什么不可告人的愿望吧？
t_en: Whoa! Looks like our honorary member is dodging the question! Could it be they've got a secret wish they don't want anyone to know?

t_ch: 那么，你有信心成为「圣杯战争」最后的赢家吗？
t_en: Well then, do you think you've got what it takes to win the Holy Grail War?

t_ch: 我势在必得。
t_en: You betcha.

t_ch: 我必赢不了。
t_en: There's no way I'm winning this.

t_ch: 我倒也不是很想赢。
t_en: I'm not that eager to win, honestly.

t_ch: 我不想赢，也不希望别人赢。
t_en: I don't want to win, and I don't want anyone else to win either.

t_ch: 你俩为什么成了采访记者？
t_en: Why are you both acting as reporters?

t_ch: 喔！荣誉队员顾左右而言他，可能藏有杀手剑！
t_en: Ooh! Dodging the question again, huh? Must be hiding a mace up their sleeve!

t_ch: 是「撒手锏」啦。
t_en: It's an "ace" up their sleeve, not "mace"!

t_ch: 对了对了，荣誉队员打算召唤哪位匹诺康尼历史上的传奇名人呢？你该不会和虎克一样，对匹诺康尼的历史完全不了解吧？
t_en: Right, right. So, honorary member, which legendary figure from history are you planning to summon? Don't tell me you're as clueless about Penacony's history as I am?

t_ch: 所以你俩到底是怎么回事？
t_en: What's your deal, anyway?

t_ch: 哎呀，{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}，咱贝洛伯格人难得走一趟天外旅行，能在异星和自己的老友不期而遇，心里面真是装满了高兴。虽说场合有些不太对，但你该不会装作不认识咱吧？
t_en: Oh, c'mon, {M#buddy}{F#girl}, it's a rare treat for us Belobogian folks to travel off-world. Running into an old friend on an alien planet? That's pure joy. Sure, the occasion might be a little weird, but don't tell me you don't recognize us?

t_ch: 我和漆黑的虎克大人约好了，这趟盛会之星的旅行，得全程录制些精彩记录给老乡们开开眼。这不，正巧碰上你来接受「匹诺康尼圣杯战争」的采访——
t_en: I promised Pitch-Dark Hook the Great we'd record every thrilling moment of this trip to the Planet of Festivities for the folks back home. And here we are — stumbling upon you being interviewed for the Penaconian Holy Grail War—

t_ch: 原来如此。我刚才就在奇怪，明明是我们频道拿下了独家采访权和播放权，怎么还有人来戗行。来人，赶快清场！
t_en: I see. I was just wondering why someone else was taking my job, considering our channel secured exclusive rights to this interview and broadcast. Security, clear the area!

t_ch: 我们可以谈谈…对，谈谈合作！
t_en: We can talk this out... How about a collaboration!

t_ch: 喂喂，苜蓿草家系就算再怎么是个钱串子，也不可能忍心向这么可爱的孩子索要授权费吧？
t_en: Hey, hey, even the money-loving Alfalfa Family wouldn't charge such a cute kid a licensing fee, right?

t_ch: 姐们，就当是做个慈善呗，让这位小朋友完成一下她的「社会实践作业」又有什么损失呢？
t_en: Miss, consider it charity! Let this kid finish her "social studies project." I mean, what's the harm in that?

t_ch: 助理，快请这两位游客一边儿呆着去！
t_en: Assistant, get these two out of here!

t_ch: 助理们不由分说涌了上来，将两位异乡游客给「请」得远远的。
t_en: The assistants surged forward without warning, "escorting" the two foreign visitors far from the scene.

t_ch: 由苜蓿草家系全力筹办的第一届匹诺康尼「圣杯战争」正在进行时，我们将全程追溯参战御主们的踪迹，并为各位带来最新鲜的一手报道。
t_en: The Alfalfa Family proudly presents the First Penaconian Holy Grail War! We'll be following the Masters every step of the way, bringing you the freshest, most exclusive coverage.

t_ch: 在对无名客进行「匹诺康尼圣杯战争」进一步采访前，让我们先进一段插播广告——
t_en: Before we continue our interview with the Nameless about the Penaconian Holy Grail War, let's cut to a quick commercial break—

t_ch: 各位…想必一定曾听说过关于「圣杯」的传说吧？
t_en: Ladies and gentlemen... I'm sure you've all heard the legend of the Holy Grail, haven't you?

t_ch: 在浩瀚寰宇中，这样的传言比比皆是：追寻者们踏上重重苦旅，只为追求一个能化心愿为现实的奇迹。
t_en: Across the vast cosmos, such tales abound: seekers embarking on grueling journeys in pursuit of a miracle capable of turning their deepest desires into reality.

t_ch: 但能有幸见证它存在的人却少之又少。嚯嗬嗬嗬！现在，我将为各位带来一个见证它的机会——
t_en: Yet, only a rare few ever have the privilege of witnessing its existence firsthand. Hohohoho! But today, I bring you all that very opportunity—

t_ch: 我将能实现一切愿望的万能许愿机——「圣杯」，带到了匹诺康尼。
t_en: I have brought the omnipotent wish-granting chalice, the Holy Grail, to Penacony!

t_ch: 当然了，许愿的资格无比珍贵。就像人生中大多数比赛那样，要想得偿所愿，就得和其他竞争者角逐！
t_en: Of course, the right to make a wish is a privilege beyond measure. And like most competitions in life, if you want to claim your reward, you'll have to fight for it!

t_ch: 「圣杯」会召唤出匹诺康尼历史上那些杰出名人的英灵，令其死而复生——而你，勇敢的逐梦客，将成为「御主」驱使这些「从者」战斗，直至幸存最后一人！
t_en: The Holy Grail will summon Heroic Spirits — legendary figures from Penacony's rich history and bring them back to life. And you, daring Dreamchasers, will become Masters, commanding these Servants in battle until only one survivor remains!

t_ch: 听起来有些暴力？担心它像泰科铵机动球竞赛那样断肢横飞、血花四溅？
t_en: Worried it sounds a bit violent? Afraid it might turn into another Taikiyan Roboball competition with limbs flying and blood spattering everywhere?

t_ch: 不必担心！请永远记住：在匹诺康尼的美梦中不存在死亡！您所见证的一切暴力，都是安全无害的梦境秀！唯独「愿望成真」是真实不虚的奖赏！
t_en: Fear not! Mark my words: in the sweet dream of Penacony, there is no such thing as death! Any violence you witness is nothing more than harmless spectacle. The only thing real... is the prize of having your wish come true!

t_ch: 毕竟，这里可是逐梦之星。在匹诺康尼，每个心怀夙愿的人都有机会实现自己的愿望。
t_en: After all, this is the planet of chasing dreams. Here in Penacony, anyone with a dream in their heart has a chance to make it a reality.

t_ch: 现在，由苜蓿草家系主办，苏乐达™赞助播出的，以梦想为赌注的残酷战场——
t_en: Now, brought to you by the Alfalfa Family and proudly sponsored by SoulGlad™, the high-stakes battlefield where dreams are on the line—

t_ch: ——匹诺康尼第一届「圣杯战争」！开幕！
t_en: The first-ever Penaconian Holy Grail War! Let the games begin!

t_ch: 此刻，在另一边，圣杯战争的举办人正饶有兴致地观赏着广告……
t_en: At the same time, the host of the Holy Grail War is sitting elsewhere watching the advert with interest...

t_ch: 太棒了，我尤其喜欢您用「幸存者」而非「胜利者」去鼓舞参战者。没什么比「活下来」这个目标更能激起一个人的斗志了。
t_en: Terrific! I loved how you used the word "survivor" instead of "victor" to rally the contestants. Nothing stirs up a person's drive quite like the goal of simply staying alive.

t_ch: 真是极富煽动力的演讲，奥帝先生。就算是{RUBY_B#公司创始人}路易斯•弗莱明{RUBY_E#}本人也没法做得更好。
t_en: What an inspiring speech, Mr. Oti. Even {RUBY_B#Founder of the IPC}Louis Fleming{RUBY_E#} himself couldn't have done it better!

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬！孩子，把抹了蜜的小嘴先收收。我看重的可不是你带给我的情绪价值。我邀请你加入，是希望你全力以赴，成为「幸存者」。
t_en: Hohoho! Easy on the sweet talk, kid. I'm not here for your flattery. I brought you in because I expect you to give it your all... and become the "survivor."

t_ch: 别怪我多嘴，奥帝先生…我当然很高兴得到您的赏识，但说到底，我只是一个微不足道的小角色，还是公司的小角色。听说您一直很不待见公司，不是吗？所以，为什么选我？
t_en: Don't get me wrong, Mr. Oti... I'm honored you picked me, but I'm just a nobody, a bottom-tier cog in the IPC. And if the rumors are true, you're not exactly a fan of the IPC. So why me?

t_ch: 我向匹诺康尼的股东和家主们发送「请柬」，欢迎他们加入到这场圣杯战争中来。有些人作出了回应，有些人却嗤之以鼻，把我精心布置的欢会当成一个商业噱头。
t_en: I sent invitations to Penacony's top shareholders and family heads, welcoming them to join this Holy Grail War. Some accepted. Others turned their noses up, dismissing my carefully orchestrated spectacle as just a marketing stunt.

t_ch: 看来，优渥的生活总会磨平大人物逐梦的锐气，而小角色就不同了。孩子，请向我重复一遍你打算向圣杯许下的愿望。
t_en: Seems like living in luxury has dulled the ambitions of the so-called elite. But underdogs like you? You're a whole different story. Now, remind me again. What wish would you make to the Grail?

t_ch: 鄙人斯科特，想登上{RUBY_B#总部干事}公司职级P<unbreak>45</unbreak>{RUBY_E#}的高位！
t_en: Me, Skott? I'm just a lowly grunt trying to climb to a {RUBY_B#Headquarters Officer}P<unbreak>45</unbreak> corporate rank{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬！真是俗不可耐的愿望，你甚至没想过要和「钻石」那样的大人物平起平坐……
t_en: Hohoho! What a delightfully mundane wish! You aren't even dreaming of rubbing shoulders with legends like "Diamond" or something...

t_ch: 但是，比起什么「我希望宇宙和平」之流的屁话，这才是真正脚踏实地的感人梦想啊！
t_en: But compared to lofty nonsense like world peace, your dream is refreshingly grounded.

t_ch: 所以，圣杯真的能实现我的愿望吗？
t_en: So, the Holy Grail... Can it really grant my wish?

t_ch: 当然是真的！孩子，烙印在手上的令咒已经证明了你的资格。你也召来了从者并见证了他的力量。
t_en: Of course it can! Kid, the Command Spell branded onto your hand is proof of your qualification. You've also summoned a Servant and witnessed their power for yourself.

t_ch: 毕竟这可是梦里。冰淇淋和酒瓶都能开口说话，乐器自动演奏。什么实现愿望的圣杯，死而复生的历史名人…倒也不太让人意外。
t_en: Well, we are in a dream world where ice cream talks, wine bottles sing, and instruments play themselves. A Grail that grants wishes and legendary figures resurrecting from history? Not so far-fetched here, I guess.

t_ch: 更重要的是，如果圣杯是真的，奥帝先生您为什么要把它分享出来，办这种…节目？我是说，要换做是我，早就闷声发大财，自己偷偷许愿了。
t_en: But here's the bigger question: If the Grail is real, why are you sharing it? Why turn this into a... show? Let's face it. If I were in your shoes, I'd keep it to myself, make my wish quietly, and get filthy rich.

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬，是个好问题！那么让我来考考你，斯科特。<color=#dbc291ff>对一个活过十个琥珀纪，从无数场阴谋和斗争中幸存下来，银行户头上躺着怎么花也花不完的财富，同时攀上了匹诺康尼顶峰的老人来说……</color>
t_en: Hohohoho! A sharp question, Skott. Let me put it this way: <color=#dbc291ff>Picture an old man who's survived ten Amber Eras, navigated countless conspiracies and power struggles, accumulated an endless fortune, and climbed to the absolute pinnacle of Penacony...</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>……人生中还有什么非实现不可的愿望？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Now, tell me. What's left for such a person to wish for?</color>

t_ch: …更多的钱和权力？谁会嫌这两样东西多呢？我不知道。您就直说吧，奥帝先生。
t_en: Uh... more money and power? No one ever thinks they've got too much of either, right? I don't know. You tell me, Mr. Oti.

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬，你的猜测已经很接近了，孩子。
t_en: Hohohoho! You're already getting close, kid.

t_ch: 区区一个愿望，又怎么能满足我？我要将「圣杯战争」打造成匹诺康尼未来的一项新兴娱乐——与逝去的历史名人会面，将他们召为从者为自己而战，最终实现梦寐以求的愿望！
t_en: But a single wish? That's far too small to satisfy me. I want to transform the Holy Grail War into an unprecedented form of entertainment in Penacony! Imagine meeting historical legends, summoning them as Servants to fight for you, and finally making your wildest dreams come true.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼将会拥有谐乐大典之外另一项吸引寰宇目光的盛会。数不清的金钱和逐梦者会涌向这里，远比过去多得多！
t_en: This will be Penacony's next grand spectacle, standing alongside the Charmony Festival as a beacon for the universe's attention. Dreamers and wealth beyond measure will pour in, far more than ever before!

t_ch: 而在这作为示范的第一届圣杯战争中，没什么比「一位小角色愿望成真」的故事更有煽动力了，明白吗？
t_en: And for the first-ever Holy Grail War, what story could be more compelling than the wish of a nobody coming true? Understand now?

t_ch: 我不会让您失望的，奥帝先生！作为一头孤狼，我有无论跌入怎么样的绝境都能幸存下来的自信。
t_en: I get it now. Don't worry, Mr. Oti. I won't let you down. As a lone wolf, I've learned how to survive, no matter how desperate things are.

t_ch: 不瞒您说，就在刚才的电视节目里，我又见到了那个数次让我跌落人生谷底的宿敌…是命运使然，{F#她}{M#他}也来到了同一片战场。
t_en: But I'm not gonna lie, Mr. Oti... Just now, during that broadcast, I saw someone. Someone who's dragged me down to rock bottom more times than I can count. Funny how fate works. {F#She's}{M#He's} here on this battlefield, too.

t_ch: 呵呵，你是说那个星穹列车无名客吗？那可是个爱出风头，实力强劲的选手。
t_en: Emm... Let me guess. You mean that Nameless from the Astral Express? A real showboat and a tough competitor, I'd say.

t_ch: 单论实力，过去的我确实不是这个怪物和{F#她}{M#他}那些同伙的对手。但是，现在的我，已经脱胎换骨了。
t_en: Yeah, in terms of pure strength? The old me couldn't compete with that monster and {M#his}{F#her} crew. But the person I am today? I'm not the same man I was back then.

t_ch: 作为御主，善用召来的历史英灵…「从者」，发挥他们名为「宝具」的特殊能力才是幸存的关键。我说的没错吧…芮克先生？
t_en: As a Master, survival hinges on using your summoned Heroic Spirits, your Servants, wisely, and utilizing their Noble Phantasms. Isn't that correct, Mr. Reca?

t_ch: 在圣杯战争结束前，请暂时用「葛瑞迪」这个名字来称呼我，{RUBY_B#御主}制片人{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: While the Holy Grail War is ongoing, please address me as Grady, {RUBY_B#Master}Producer{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 在圣杯战争结束前，他应当用「Assassin」这个代号来称呼你。
t_en: Actually, during this War, he should call you by the code name "Assassin."

t_ch: 你说的不错，葛瑞迪这个名字在如今的匹诺康尼一文不值，还是不提为妙。
t_en: You're right. In the current Penacony, the name Grady carries no weight. Best not to bring it up.

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬，好不容易迎来了第二次人生，该把脆弱的自尊心收收了，老朋友。
t_en: Hohohoho! You got a chance at a second life. It's best to let go of fragile pride, old friend.

t_ch: 名字代表了你的过往，也会揭露你的弱点。把自己的信息从竞争对手面前藏起来，这才是聪明人的做法。
t_en: Names tie you to your past. They also expose your weaknesses. A wise competitor hides their true self from the opposition.

t_ch: 听好了，我用作品署名权和「体验英灵的记忆」作为条件，才换来{RUBY_B#芮克先生}那位导演{RUBY_E#}自愿{RUBY_B#灵基融合}充当你的载体{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Listen up: I had to offer him film credits and the chance to experience a Heroic Spirit's memories just to convince {RUBY_B#Mr. Reca}that director{RUBY_E#} to {RUBY_B#Spirit Origin Fusion}act as your vessel{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 你不是一直想创作一部惊世骇俗、留名青史的杰作吗——关于这点，你和芮克先生都有同样的追求。别浪费我为你们创造的条件……
t_en: You've always dreamed of creating a masterpiece that would shake the world and cement your name in history, haven't you? On that point, you and Mr. Reca share the same goal. Don't waste the opportunity I've created for you...

t_ch: 去记录下这场圣杯战争中那些参战者的经历，同时帮助你身旁这位野心勃勃的年轻人得偿所愿！
t_en: Go record the stories of the participants in this Holy Grail War, and help this ambitious young man by your side fulfill his wish!

t_ch: 按照御主的吩咐，我的宝具早已瞄准了那些您邀请的对象。我可以将他们的动向随时呈现在两位面前的。
t_en: Following my Master's command, my Noble Phantasm has already been trained on those you invited. I can show their movements to you both in real time.

t_ch: 干得好，Assassin。有这样方便的能力，我们一定能占得先机！
t_en: Well done, Assassin. With an ability like this, we're sure to stay ahead of the competition!

t_ch: 我的制片人，时间宝贵，还是快点看看其他参战者的情况吧。
t_en: Producer, time's ticking. Let's hurry and check out the other participants!

t_ch: 奥帝先生，我们这样提前召唤出「从者」，会不会有点…违反规则？
t_en: Mr. Oti, summoning a Servant this early... Isn't that a little against the rules?

t_ch: 噗哈哈哈哈，我还真没想到，我们的制片人这么在乎「规则」！
t_en: Hahaha! I didn't realize our producer cared so much about the rules!

t_ch: 当然要在乎规则了！不顾规则乱搞一气是很过瘾，但如果被人事后找麻烦，吃亏的可还是自己！
t_en: You bet I care about the rules! It's fun to break them in the heat of the moment, but if trouble comes knocking afterward, we're the ones who get hurt!

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬…放心吧，本次「圣杯战争」规则的最终解释权，都归我老奥帝所有。
t_en: Hohoho... Rest assured. For this Holy Grail War, I have the final say on all the rules.

t_ch: 将忆泡当成请柬，真有匹诺康尼当地的特色。
t_en: Using memory bubbles as invitations... a very Penaconian thing to do.

t_ch: 不过更有匹诺康尼特色的还得是这个——「能实现一切愿望的万能许愿机」。听听，像不像某个拙劣的商业骗局？还是在雅利洛-VI这样的穷乡僻壤才能骗到人的诡计。
t_en: But what's even more Penaconian is this "omnipotent wish-granting chalice." Listen to that. Doesn't it sound like a cheap business scam? Only someone from a backwater place like Jarilo-VI could be fooled by a trick like that.

t_ch: ……老奥帝端上了「甘露」，那么在杯中兑入的「鸩毒」又是什么呢？
t_en: ...Old Oti's serving up the "nectar," but what kind of "poison" is mixed into the cup?

t_ch: 翡翠女士，你该不会真的在琢磨这个老头子玩什么把戏吧？
t_en: Madam Jade, don't tell me you're actually trying to figure out what kind of game this old man is playing?

t_ch: 为什么不呢？匹诺康尼的商界巨头抛下过去和气生财的形象，向家系之主和股东们发出邀约，进行这场梦中的角逐，允诺事后兑现一个你心底无法实现的愿望…这不是很有趣吗？
t_en: Why not? Penacony's biggest tycoon throws away his usual image of polite deal-making to invite the Family heads and shareholders to participate in this dream contest, promising afterward to grant a wish that someone could never fulfill on their own... Isn't that fascinating?

t_ch: 有趣？位高权重的人生命每一分钟都价值连城，谁会愿意放下身段陪他消遣这样的把戏？如果事后证明所谓的圣杯只是纯粹的商业噱头，事情恐怕会变得更有趣喽。
t_en: Fascinating? People with real power wouldn't waste a second of their time on something like this. Who would lower themselves just to entertain him? And if this so-called Holy Grail turns out to be nothing but a marketing gimmick, things are gonna get really interesting.

t_ch: 老奥帝显然明白这点。是什么样的收益让他愿意押上苜蓿草家系的商誉？
t_en: Old Oti knows that better than anyone. So what kind of return is big enough for him to risk the Alfalfa Family's entire reputation?

t_ch: 所以，您认为这个「万能的许愿机」确有其事。
t_en: So, you believe this "omnipotent wish-granting chalice" might actually be real.

t_ch: 纵观寰宇，通过某种方式攫取命途力量，达成「奇迹」的仪式并不罕见。我与人签订的「典贷契约」，不也是一种助人心想事成的仪式吗？至于我们获赐的基石…更是如此。
t_en: Across the cosmos, ceremonies that tap into a Path's power to create "miracles" aren't uncommon. The Credit Contracts I make with people. Aren't they a form of wish fulfillment, too? And even more so... are the Cornerstones we received.

t_ch: 这么说起来，您有兴趣接下这封请柬？
t_en: So, does that mean you're interested in accepting the invitation?

t_ch: 我对「圣杯战争」确实感兴趣——但不是对圣杯本身，而是对那些会被「奇迹」打动，加入角逐的人。
t_en: I am interested in the Holy Grail War, but not in the Grail itself. What intrigues me are the people who'd join this War for a so-called "miracle."

t_ch: 将自己的愿望寄托在不知来历的圣杯上…就像一只全身涂满了油的狮子，非要表演钻火圈。
t_en: Placing your hopes and dreams on some unknown Holy Grail... it's like dousing yourself in oil and diving straight through a ring of fire.

t_ch: …前景不容乐观，但场面一定精彩。
t_en: Doesn't look promising. But it'll be one hell of a show.

t_ch: 不过，这正是一个观察与结交的好机会。「圣杯」能满足的不过是一个幸运儿，而「慈玉典押」…不，公司的「战略投资部」却能满足他们所有人的愿望。
t_en: And more than that, it's the perfect opportunity to watch and make connections. Only one lucky fool will walk away with the Grail. But the Bonajade Exchange. No, the IPC's Strategic Investment Department can grant everyone's wishes.

t_ch: 我们未来的战略需要更多朋友。砂金，你会接下请柬加入这场「圣杯战争」，对吧？
t_en: Our future strategy calls for more allies. Aventurine, you're going to accept the invitation and join the Holy Grail War, aren't you?

t_ch: 我就知道，我和匹诺康尼缘分不浅。只是把{RUBY_B#令咒}参赛资格{RUBY_E#}烙印在身上的方式，真让人有些不快……
t_en: I knew it. Penacony and I just can't seem to stay apart. But I have to say, the way they {RUBY_B#Command Spell}mark{RUBY_E#} the participants... not exactly pleasant.

t_ch: 说到「朋友」，一位{RUBY_B#波提欧}「潜在的友人」{RUBY_E#}也回到了匹诺康尼。很显然，我们不可能与他建立任何官方往来…但好在他之前曾和你交换过情报，建立了些许信任。
t_en: Speaking of allies, a {RUBY_B#Boothill}certain someone{RUBY_E#} has returned to Penacony. Obviously, we can't have any official dealings with him... but since you've exchanged intel before, there's a little trust between you two.

t_ch: 我希望你能趁这次「圣杯战争」的机会，和他加深一些「友谊」。
t_en: I want you to use this Holy Grail War as a chance to strengthen that trust.

t_ch: 如您所愿。
t_en: As you wish.

t_ch: 这是…「战略投资部」的人也会加入？！
t_en: Wait... You're telling me the Strategic Investment Department is joining, too!?

t_ch: 斯科特先生这是怕了？作为「市场开拓部」的成员，他们的手可管不到你这边来吧？
t_en: What's wrong, Mr. Skott? As a member of the Marketing Development Department, their reach shouldn't extend to you, right?

t_ch: 没错，奥帝先生。何况有您给我撑腰，就算是路易斯•弗莱明……
t_en: That's right, Mr. Oti. Besides, with you backing me up, even Louis Fleming...

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬，咱们就让路易斯•弗莱明好好歇歇吧。
t_en: Hohohoho... Let's just let Louis Fleming rest for now.

t_ch: 斯科特先生，公司不过是广阔银河中的一个选择罢了。有时候我们需要为自己争取一飞冲天的机会。我看好你喔！
t_en: Mr. Skott, the IPC is just one of many choices in this vast cosmos. Sometimes, you need to fight for your own shot at greatness. I've got high hopes for you.

t_ch: 「帕姆先生」，好久不见了～
t_en: "Mr. Pom-Pom," it's been a while~

t_ch: 哈哈，真他宝贝的好笑。姐们，我知道自己的身份比较敏感…但在流梦礁，还是直呼我的大名吧。
t_en: Hah! Now that's forkin' hilarious. Girl, I know my identity's a little... touchy. But here in Dreamflux Reef, just call me by my name, alright?

t_ch: 打从猴子在这儿大闹一场后，咱俩确实有好一阵子没见面了。经纪人怎么会放任你这样的大明星在这儿搞街边演出？
t_en: It sure has been a while since we last saw each other. Not since that monkey raised hell here. How'd your agent even let a big-time star like you put on a street performance like this?

t_ch: 真正的歌者不会只向靠近舞台的观众献唱。
t_en: A true singer doesn't only perform for the ones who can afford front-row seats.

t_ch: 倒是波提欧先生，你出现在这儿，代表着流梦礁又要发生什么大事了吗？
t_en: But you, Mr. Boothill, your appearance here means something big's about to go down again in Dreamflux Reef, doesn't it?

t_ch: 可别把我说得像是什么灾难警报装置啊！你知道「圣杯战争」吧？广告到处都是，匹诺康尼没人不知道吧？
t_en: Hey now, don't go makin' me sound like some kind of walking disaster siren! You've heard of the Holy Grail War, right? The ads are everywhere. Nobody in Penacony could've missed it.

t_ch: 所以，波提欧先生是来参加「圣杯战争」的吗？
t_en: Wait... are you telling me you're joining the Holy Grail War?

t_ch: 嗯？你怎么这么惊讶？我是不应该参加吗？
t_en: Huh? Why do you sound so shocked? Am I not supposed to?

t_ch: 我当然不是这个意思…又或者说，我就是这个意思……
t_en: That's not what I meant... or maybe that is what I meant...

t_ch: …据我所知，这场「圣杯战争」，理应只向家系之主、公司高管和星穹列车发出了邀请函——换言之，就是匹诺康尼的「决策者」们。
t_en: As far as I know, only The Family heads, IPC execs, and the Astral Express crew received invitations to this Holy Grail War — basically, the "decision-makers" of Penacony.

t_ch: 我「帕姆先生」不就是星穹列车的成员吗？
t_en: And ain't I, "Pom-Pom," a member of the Astral Express?

t_ch: 我不是那个意思啦！
t_en: That's not what I am saying!

t_ch: 哈哈，开个玩笑。不知道发了什么神经，那个老可爱给流梦礁也送来了一份邀请函。米凯担心事情会变得像过去那样——上面人找乐子，底下人挨刀子。
t_en: Hah, relax, I'm just messin' with you. For some reason, that old man went and sent an invitation to Dreamflux Reef too. Micah figured things might go down the same way they always do — the folks up top have their fun, and the people below take the hits.

t_ch: 所以，他把请柬转送给了我，说是我会感兴趣。
t_en: So he sent me the invite, said I might be interested.

t_ch: 我明白，米凯先生可以说是在变相向您求助。
t_en: I see... So Mr. Micah's asking for your help, in his own way.

t_ch: 合理，我高低也算是两次拯救过匹诺康尼的英雄人物啊！不管是星期日还是猴子……
t_en: Makes sense. I mean, after all I'm a two-time hero of Penacony! Sunday, the monkeys...

t_ch: 嗐，瞧我这欠爱的破嘴，跟你提这茬干什么？对不起。我对什么呜呜伯爱的「圣杯」没兴趣，但「圣杯战争」？这听起来太他宝贝的危险了。
t_en: Ahh, dadgum, my stupid mouth. Why'd I bring that up? Sorry. Listen, I don't care about any muddle-fudgin' Holy Grail, but a Holy Grail War? Now that sounds dangerous.

t_ch: 的确，我也有同感。
t_en: Yeah... I get that feeling too.

t_ch: 把死人从坟里召唤出来彼此厮杀，然后赢家通吃？这鼻行兽养的比赛规则实在和我爱不到一个壶里去，典型的公司小可爱思维。
t_en: Summoning dead guys from their graves to duke it out, winner takes all? That sounds like something those IPC shirtbags would come up with. No heckin' way I vibe with that kinda setup.

t_ch: 既然接受了米凯的求助，我也要全力以赴，免得事情闹到收不了场。
t_en: But since I took Micah's request, I gotta see this through. Make sure things don't spiral out of control.

t_ch: 行了，该准备准备了。接下来要按请柬的指示「召唤英灵」了。回见！
t_en: Alright, time to get prepped. Up next, per the invite's instructions... summoning a Heroic Spirit. See ya!

t_ch: 这个人是…通缉犯波提欧？！
t_en: That guy... That's the wanted criminal Boothill!?

t_ch: 奥帝先生，您…您能不能撤销他的资格？让通缉犯参与「圣杯战争」，实在太危险了…万一他许愿让公司员工全员暴毙，那不就完蛋了！
t_en: Mr. Oti, sir, c—can't you revoke his qualification? Letting a criminal join the Holy Grail War is way too dangerous! What if he wishes for every single IPC employee to drop dead? That'd be the end of us!

t_ch: 孩子，你这是默认这位牛仔会打赢圣杯战争？这么灭自己威风可不好啊。
t_en: Kid, you're basically admitting you think that cowboy's going to win the whole thing. Where's your confidence? You gotta keep your head in the game.

t_ch: 一旦接受了请柬，烙印了令咒，身为御主的资格便是无法撤回的。
t_en: Once you accept the invitation and are branded with the Command Spells, your qualification as a Master can't be revoked.

t_ch: 那就请赶快派出猎犬，对这个通缉犯重拳出击！
t_en: Then we should send out the Bloodhounds and take that criminal down immediately!

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬～可是那位波提欧先生说的没错，他是两次挽救过匹诺康尼的英雄。贸然对他进行围剿，不是什么妥善处置呐。
t_en: Hohoho~ But Mr. Boothill wasn't wrong, was he? He's saved Penacony twice before. If we jump the gun and hunt him down now, that wouldn't be very... tactful.

t_ch: …斯科特先生为何如此忌惮这个家伙？
t_en: ...Why are you so scared of this guy, Skott?

t_ch: 呃…因为一些小小的个人恩怨，他对我们部门的同事下手时格外残暴……
t_en: Uh... let's just say there's some personal history. He's got a, uh... particularly brutal way of handling my department's people...

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬～这不正是你为同僚们报仇雪恨的好机会？请您尽力获得胜利，将这位高傲的牛仔狠狠击落吧。
t_en: Hohoho~ Then isn't this your perfect shot at vengeance? Win this war, and you can bring that cocky cowboy crashing down yourself.

t_ch: 唉…放心吧，奥帝先生，别说一个小小的波提欧了，只要陷阱和计谋准备得充分，就算是路易斯•弗莱明也不过是待宰羔羊！
t_en: Heh... Don't worry, Mr. Oti. Forget a small fry like Boothill. If the traps are set right and the schemes are airtight, even Louis Fleming wouldn't be able to escape!

t_ch: 好！好！好！嚯嗬嗬嗬嗬！你不择手段的样子有我年轻时的风采！
t_en: Good! Good! GOOD! Hohohohoho! That ruthless streak of yours reminds me of my younger days!

t_ch: 又是哪位乘客乱扔的垃圾？等我抓到他，一定要让他罚抄一百遍列车卫生守则！
t_en: Who the heck keeps tossing garbage around? The next passenger Pom-Pom catches littering is going to have to write down the train's sanitation rules, a hundred times!

t_ch: 嗯？匹诺康尼第一届「圣杯战争」…诚邀星穹列车参与？这样凑热闹的事情，{NICKNAME}乘客也许会感兴趣帕。
t_en: Huh? The First Penaconian Holy Grail War? Inviting the Astral Express to join? Sounds like the kind of spectacle that might interest you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 唔，{F#她}{M#他}似乎不在。不如转交给三月乘客和丹恒乘客——欸！帕姆的手为什么染上了一块涂鸦！
t_en: Hmm... Seems like {F#s}he's not around. Maybe I should pass this to March and Dan Heng instead — wait, why is there graffiti on Pom-Pom's hand!?

t_ch: 擦…擦不干净？到底是谁搞的恶作剧帕？
t_en: Ugh... it won't come off!? Who's behind this prank?

t_ch: 啧，真是伦常颠倒！星穹列车上的动物竟然也能获得参赛资格……？
t_en: Tsk, the world's turned upside down... Even the animals on the Astral Express can enter this tournament!?

t_ch: 奥帝先生，需要我优先处理一下吗？
t_en: Mr. Oti, should I take care of this first?

t_ch: 不必了~让这种选手参赛，不也很有趣嘛？
t_en: No need~ Letting a contestant like them compete just makes things even more interesting!

t_ch: 舞台布置妥当，角色们也都纷纷上场了。不过，御主，对你我来说，这场「圣杯战争」似乎有一些潜在的隐忧啊。
t_en: The stage is set, and the characters are all in position. But, Master, for you and me, this Holy Grail War comes with a few... complications.

t_ch: 这一次的竞争对手确实都不是省油的灯。
t_en: Yeah, the competition this time is no joke.

t_ch: 不，我的意思是…纵观这批登场角色的前史，主要可以分为三类：{RUBY_B#砂金}和你的部门有着竞争关系的{RUBY_E#}、{RUBY_B#波提欧}和你的部门有个人恩怨的{RUBY_E#}、{RUBY_B#无名客}和你个人不共戴天的{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: No, what I mean is... If you look at the backstories of the participants, they generally fall into three categories: {RUBY_B#Aventurine}those who are in direct competition with your department{RUBY_E#}, {RUBY_B#Boothill}those who have a personal grudge against your department{RUBY_E#}, and {RUBY_B#Nameless}those who see you as their sworn enemy{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 换言之，从叙事学角度来讲，我们目前所发现的每一位参战者，都会在第一时间将你作为目标。
t_en: In other words, from a narrative standpoint, every single participant we've identified so far is likely to target you first.

t_ch: ——是我们。这*公司粗口*叫「隐忧」吗？我还不知道这里每个人都跟我有仇？
t_en: It's us. You call that a *IPC profanity* "complication"? Like I didn't already know everyone here has a bone to pick with me?

t_ch: 一点小小的个人经验：并不是每个人都和你「有仇」，孩子。你要学会区分「利益冲突」和「私人恩怨」。
t_en: A little personal wisdom: Not everyone is your enemy, kid. Learn to separate "conflict of interest" from "personal vendetta."

t_ch: 这两者最核心的区别在于，前者有得谈，后者没得谈。所以，如果我是你……
t_en: And the key difference? The first one can be negotiated. The second? Not so much. So if I were you...

t_ch: …就会优先处理「私人恩怨」。哼哼，谢谢您，奥帝先生，我受益良多。
t_en: You'd deal with the personal vendettas first. Heh, heh. Much appreciated, Mr. Oti. I'll keep that in mind.

t_ch: 真是巧妙的故事回环，往日狭路相逢的角色注定要在「圣杯战争」的舞台上分出胜负。
t_en: What a poetic twist... Old rivals now destined to settle the score on the Holy Grail War's stage.

t_ch: 哼哼，所幸，我抽到了你这张上上签，Assassin！凭借你的宝具，我们应该能占得先机……
t_en: Lucky for me, I drew the best possible card: Assassin! With your Noble Phantasm, we should have the upper hand...

t_ch: 若要说占得先机，光得知对方要做什么恐怕还不够。就像光有剧本，却不付诸拍摄可不行。我必须提醒你，御主，你最讨厌的那位无名客此刻正在准备召唤从者的仪式……
t_en: That said, knowing your opponent's next move isn't enough. It's like having a script but never actually shooting the film. Let me remind you, Master. Your least-favorite Nameless is preparing the ritual to summon a Servant right now...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，{NICKNAME}，老子必须先把你给料理了，以免夜长梦多。
t_en: Heh heh, {NICKNAME}, I gotta take care of you first. Can't have any loose ends hanging around.

t_ch: 那些有意接受「请柬」并打算加入圣杯战争的参战者，已经时刻在我的宝具注视之下了。制片人，你打算先看看哪个呢？
t_en: The participants who have accepted the invitation and plan to join the Holy Grail War are already under my Noble Phantasm's watch. So producer, which one do you want to check out first?

t_ch: 接下来，就是最激动人心的时刻了！{NICKNAME}将踏入召唤阵，念动咒语，召唤出匹诺康尼历史上伟大的灵魂…之一！
t_en: Next up is the moment we've all been waiting for! {NICKNAME} will step in the summoning circle, chant the spell, and summon one of the greatest souls in Penaconian history!

t_ch: 究竟会是哪位匹诺康尼的历史名人会受召降临呢？是筑造梦境的格拉克斯？还是最初的猎犬？让我们拭目以待！
t_en: Who will it be? The legendary dreamweaver Glaux? Or perhaps the first Bloodhound? Let's find out!

t_ch: 气氛都烘托到这里了，去召唤阵那里开始仪式吧。
t_en: The stage is set. Now, step into the summoning circle and begin the ritual.

t_ch: 嗯…开始吧。
t_en: Alright... let's begin.

t_ch: 汝之身躯托吾麾下；吾之命运附汝剑上。
t_en: Heed my words. My will creates your body, and your sword creates my destiny.

t_ch: 应列车之鸣笛，顺香香酥酥脆脆的帕姆派而来者！
t_en: If you heed the Express's whistle and obey the Crispy Crumbly Pom-Pom Pie, then answer!

t_ch: 我并非为成就世上一切善念而来，却注定为摧毁世上一切恶念而死。
t_en: I have not come to bring all the good in the world, but I am fated to perish, defeating all evil in the world.

t_ch: 自琥珀王之石心中流溢而出的七个魂灵……
t_en: Seven souls flowing from the Amber Lord's stony heart...

t_ch: 穿越平衡之轮出现吧，天秤的守护者！
t_en: Come forth from the circle of binding, Guardian of the Scales!

t_ch: 你等待了一会儿，但什么都没有发生。
t_en: You waited for a while, but nothing happened.

t_ch: …所以，从者呢？
t_en: So... Where's the Servant?

t_ch: 没有光污染、没有激动人心的音乐、没有从天而降的灵魂，只有尴尬的沉默。
t_en: There was no light pollution, no exciting music, and no soul that came down from the sky. There was only an awkward silence.

t_ch: 我从者呢？？？
t_en: Where's my Servant???

t_ch: 真想把你这个丢人现眼的时刻永远记录下来，当成传家宝子子孙孙传下去。
t_en: Oh, I wish I could capture this pathetic little moment of yours and pass it down as a family heirloom for generations.

t_ch: 本来我还担心你会抽中什么了不得的幸运签，唤来哪个可怕的从者——没想到啊没想到，你是个神憎鬼厌的家伙。
t_en: I was actually worried you'd luck out and summon some terrifying Servant... but turns out, you're just plain cursed!

t_ch: 和你一比，拥有从者的我都显得不够「孤狼」了！要不这名号借你用两天吧～哼哼哈哈哈！
t_en: Compared to you, even I seem less of a "lone wolf" now that I have a Servant. Maybe I should let you borrow the title for a couple of days. Bwahaha!

t_ch: 斯科特，怎么又是你？
t_en: Skott!? You again?

t_ch: 斯科特先生？怎么又是你？怎么老是你？
t_en: Skott!? You again? Why is it always you?

t_ch: 当然是为了找你一雪前耻——你以为我会这么说吗？别把自己想得太重要了！
t_en: What, you think I came all this way just to get payback? Oh, don't flatter yourself!

t_ch: 我只是来实现梦想的！我一点都不在乎你！真的，你来不来参赛都不影响我的心态！完全不影响！完全不在乎！真的！
t_en: I'm here to chase my dream! I don't care about you! At all! Seriously, your existence has zero impact on my mental state! None! Nada! Not at all! I'm completely unbothered! Totally!

t_ch: 你就是我的从者吗？
t_en: Are you my Servant?

t_ch: 试问，你就是我的从者吗？
t_en: Tell me, are you my Servant?

t_ch: 我才不是你的从者！白痴，我是你的对手！混蛋，你该不会认不得我吧！
t_en: What!? Obviously not! I'm your opponent, fool! Do you seriously not recognize me!?

t_ch: 没有从者你也不是我对手…
t_en: Even without a Servant, you're no match for me.

t_ch: 你该不会以为没有成功召唤从者，你就能偷袭打败我？
t_en: You think you can take a cheap shot and beat me just because my summoning failed?

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，让我教教你规则的重要性。这可是「圣杯战争」！拥有从者的优势和没有从者相比，就像穿着动力甲和赤膊上战场的区别！
t_en: Listen up, {M#lad}{F#girlie}, let me teach you why rules matter. This is the Holy Grail War! Having a Servant versus fighting alone? That's the difference between wearing power armor and charging in shirtless!

t_ch: 就不浪费时间了，得趁召唤仪式失败的最佳时机，让你连本带利尝尝过去我受的所有屈辱。
t_en: Let's not waste time. Your summoning ritual just flopped. This is the perfect moment to serve you every ounce of humiliation you once dealt me, with interest.

t_ch: 不过我这人最讲规矩，既然你没召唤出从者，那我也不用从者来揍你…兄弟们！上！
t_en: But hey, I'm a fair guy. Since you failed to summon a Servant... I won't use mine to beat you either. Boys, go get 'em!

t_ch: 这些废物点心比我想的还不顶事，总之，先撤退吧。
t_en: These useless mooks are even worse than I thought. Anyways, just fall back for now.

t_ch: 斯科特先生，你该不会只有这两下子吧？
t_en: Skott, that's all you've got?

t_ch: 呵，你还真有胆子追过来啊，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: You really had the guts to chase me down, huh, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 可惜呀可惜，你的「圣杯战争」之路，就要到此为止了。
t_en: What a shame. Your Holy Grail War ends right here.

t_ch: 目标已经进入包围网了！准备好了吗？Assassin！
t_en: Target is in position! Ready, Assassin?

t_ch: 随时可以拍摄！我的制片人！
t_en: Ready to roll, producer!

t_ch: 血浆横飞的星间砍杀喜剧，Action！
t_en: A blood-splattered, interstellar slasher comedy. Action!

t_ch: 那投枪…是怎么回事？怎么清场了？
t_en: That spear... What just happened? It wiped the field!

t_ch: 该不会这家伙是你召来的从者吧！Assassin…快、快想想办法！
t_en: Don't tell me this guy's your Servant! Assassin, do something!

t_ch: 有人搅局。制片人，正面作战可不是我的长处。这个来片场闹事的家伙显然不好惹。还是先迈动您高贵的腿，开跑吧！
t_en: Someone's crashed the set. Producer, I'm not built for front-line combat. And that guy is bad news. So, how about we take the smart approach and run?

t_ch: 啧，让御主也灵体化了吗？Assassin这个职阶，怎么总是有些让人不快的本领。
t_en: Hmph. So he put his Master into spirit form too. Assassin-class... always such a pain in the neck.

t_ch: 你就是我的从者吗？
t_en: Are you my Servant?

t_ch: 我看起来像是吗？
t_en: Do I look like it?

t_ch: 谢谢你救了我。
t_en: Thanks for saving me.

t_ch: 哦，不客气。我的本意是连你一起打倒的。
t_en: Oh, don't thank me just yet. My plan was to take you out next.

t_ch: 别打了，我们去钓鱼吧。
t_en: Stop fighting! Let's go fishing instead.

t_ch: 不错的提议。不过，我这不是钓上了你这条大鱼吗？
t_en: Not a bad idea. But you see, I just hooked a big one.

t_ch: 你可以叫我Lancer。
t_en: You can call me Lancer.

t_ch: 这当然不是我的「真名」，但你马上就要退出这个游戏了，所以我的「真名」对你也没什么价值。
t_en: That's not my True Name, of course, but since you'll be out of the game soon... well, does it even matter?

t_ch: 再见了，召唤不出从者的御主。
t_en: Farewell, Master-with-no-Servant.

t_ch: 是你？哼，真是糟糕的重逢。
t_en: You? Tch... What a terrible reunion.

t_ch: 我也是这么想的，Lancer。有我在此，绝不会坐视你伤害我的御主。
t_en: That's what I was thinking too, Lancer. But as long as I stand here, I won't let you lay a hand on my Master.

t_ch: 好好庆幸吧，{F#小姑娘}{M#小子}。你中头奖了。
t_en: Well, kid... you just hit the jackpot.

t_ch: 现在应该安全了，御主。你没受伤吧？
t_en: It's safe now, Master. Are you hurt?

t_ch: 你们认识？
t_en: Wait, you two know each other?

t_ch: 在过去的圣杯战争中，有过几次交手。虽然我们经常立场相悖，但他人还不错。
t_en: We've crossed swords a few times in past Holy Grail Wars. Our allegiances often clashed, but... he's not a bad person.

t_ch: 你就是我的从者吗？
t_en: Are you my Servant?

t_ch: 虽然维持我存续的力量似乎并非从您身体流出，但…我确实能感受到和你之间存在着连结。
t_en: While the energy sustaining me does not seem to originate from you... there is, without a doubt, a connection between us.

t_ch: 是的，您就是我的御主，而我是您的从者。
t_en: Yes, you are my Master. And I am your Servant.

t_ch: 你似乎…并不属于这个世界。
t_en: You don't... seem to be from this world.

t_ch: 我也察觉到了。
t_en: I've noticed that as well.

t_ch: 你是匹诺康尼的人吗？
t_en: Are you from Penacony?

t_ch: 不，我并非此世的英灵。我所属的世界里，从未听过叫做「匹诺康尼」的国家。
t_en: No, I am no Heroic Spirit of this land. I've never even heard of a nation called Penacony in my world.

t_ch: 在召唤现界的时刻，圣杯给予了我参与这场圣杯战争所必需的知识。但这些知识中却充满了难以理解的概念。这里似乎和我熟悉的世界…完全不一样。
t_en: The Holy Grail granted me the knowledge I need to participate in this war, but much of it makes no sense. This world... is completely foreign to me.

t_ch: 啊，忘了自我介绍，你可以称呼我身为从者时的职阶「Saber」，也可以称呼吾名阿尔托莉雅•潘德拉贡。我曾是卡美洛的骑士之王。我猜，你应该对这个地方也一无所知。
t_en: Ah, I forgot to introduce myself. You may call me by my Servant class, Saber, or by my True Name, Altria Pendragon. I was once the King of Knights in Camelot. I assume you know nothing about this place either?

t_ch: 的确一无所知。
t_en: Not a thing.

t_ch: 似乎从最原初的部分开始，我们就已经分属于两个完全不同的世界了。
t_en: It seems that, from the very foundation of our existence, we hail from two entirely different worlds.

t_ch: 我们这里也有个「卡美洛」。
t_en: We do have a Camelot here, actually.

t_ch: 哦？看来不同的世界之间，总会有一些微妙的共通点。你们这里的「卡美洛」还好吗？
t_en: Oh? Seems like different worlds have their share of strange parallels. Tell me, does your Camelot still stand?

t_ch: 很不幸，已经灭亡了。倒是还有一些遗民。我有个叫{RUBY_B#桂乃芬}格妮薇儿{RUBY_E#}的朋友就是从那儿来的，她还有个哥哥名叫{RUBY_B#郎世乐}兰斯洛特{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Unfortunately... no. It was completely destroyed. But there are still survivors. I have a friend from there, {RUBY_B#Guinaifen}Guinevere{RUBY_E#}. She's got an older brother named {RUBY_B#Langshile}Lancelot{RUBY_E#}.
Wait, what???

t_ch: 格妮薇儿，的哥哥…兰斯洛特？？？是那种…亲生的哥哥吗？
t_en: Guinevere's... brother... Lancelot? You mean like, biologically related?

t_ch: 算了，还是暂时将这个问题放一边比较好…看来不同的世界之间，差异真的很大。
t_en: You know what? Let's just put a pin in that for now. Clearly, our worlds have some serious differences.

t_ch: 为了让这位从者理解这个沐浴在忆质的梦中世界，你大谈特谈起星际旅行和银河地理。
t_en: To help this Servant understand this dream world bathing in memoria, you go on a lecture about interstellar travel and cosmic geography.

t_ch: 忽然传来了一阵雷鸣般的「咕噜」声…显然，眼前的女战士饿坏了。
t_en: Only to suddenly hear a thunderous rumble... It seems the lady knight before you is starving.

t_ch: 打雷了？
t_en: Was that thunder?

t_ch: 你饿了？
t_en: You hungry?

t_ch: …不好意思，刚见面就露出了这种窘态。
t_en: Forgive me. I must seem rather undignified.

t_ch: 你也许无法察觉，但你所在的这个世界，魔力的含量微乎其微。
t_en: You may not realize it, but this world has almost no magical energy.

t_ch: 而我这样完全不为人所知的异界英灵，更是无法因「知名」或「神秘」而获得额外的力量…如此一来，想要维持战斗力…就只能依靠巨量的能量摄入。
t_en: And as a Heroic Spirit from a completely unknown realm, I can't draw power from "fame" or Mystics. So, to maintain my combat strength... I need a lot of energy intake.

t_ch: 我的意思是，御主，你知道这附近有什么食物份量充足的餐厅吗？
t_en: Master. Do you know anywhere around here that serves large portions?

t_ch: 其实你不必解释那么多…
t_en: You really don't have to explain all that...

t_ch: 抱歉，但对初次见面的御主提出的第一个要求竟然是「大吃一顿」…这实在有失为王的仪态。
t_en: My apologies. To think the first request I make of my new Master is to... devour a feast. Hardly befitting of a king's dignity.

t_ch: 说到份量，你要是不挑口味…
t_en: Well, if you're not picky about the food...

t_ch: 没关系，我对口味的要求本就不高。何况，饥饿就是最好的调味料。
t_en: Not at all. I've never been particular about taste. Besides, hunger is the best seasoning.

t_ch: 走吧，我带你去吃本地特色的「天环翅堡」！你们的世界肯定没这道菜～
t_en: Come on! I'll take you to try the local specialty, Halovian Winged Burgers! No way your world has that!

t_ch: 与此同时，黄金的时刻。
t_en: At the same time, in the Golden Hour.

t_ch: 斯科特专员！你可没说过对手实力有这么强啊？
t_en: Specialist Skott! You never said the enemy was this strong!

t_ch: 这根本不是我们能对付的！记得帮我报加班费！
t_en: This is way out of our league! Don't forget to file my overtime pay!

t_ch: 你们这群混蛋，给我撑住！
t_en: You idiots! Hold the line!

t_ch: 接下来，换我来抓你咯？
t_en: Guess it's my turn to hunt you down now.

t_ch: 这些是…什么东西？
t_en: What... are these things?

t_ch: 不过是些不值一提的「群演」罢了。
t_en: Just some disposable extras. Nothing special.

t_ch: 给我放尊重一点，这可是我宝贵的一部分！
t_en: Watch your mouth! They're very valuable to me!

t_ch: 在我喊「Cut」之前，这些「群演」会永无休止地表演下去。
t_en: Until I yell "Cut," these extras will keep performing forever!

t_ch: 哼哼哈哈哈，打不死你也累死你！
t_en: Bwahaha! If I can't kill you, I'll exhaust you!

t_ch: 真是受不了，外行人居然在打圣杯战争，这哪是未来世界，简直就是世界末日。
t_en: Unbelievable. Amateurs playing at a Holy Grail War? This isn't a future world, it's the end of the world.

t_ch: 混账，你是谁啊？
t_en: Who the heck are you!?

t_ch: 给我看好了，臭外行。这种时候，你就应该…这样做！
t_en: Watch and learn, rookie. At a time like this, you should be... doing this!

t_ch: 我一向是个在不运与强运的狭间中游走的赌徒，但就算对我来说，这「抽牌」的结果也有些……
t_en: I've always been a gambler, teetering between bad luck and incredible fortune. But even for me, this draw of the cards is a bit...

t_ch: 我倒还挺喜欢你那位从者的，这种讲原则、守边界的合作伙伴，作为结盟对象，至少比你们这些一肚子坏水的值得信赖。
t_en: Still, I have to say, I kinda like your Servant. A partner who plays by the rules and knows his boundaries? I'd take that over you schemers any day. At least he's trustworthy.

t_ch: 这的确是个不得了的从者，但……
t_en: No doubt, he's an incredible Servant. But...

t_ch: …你是匹诺康尼历史上的谁来着？
t_en: Who exactly are you in Penaconian history?

t_ch: 亲爱的御主，我看起来和你们这个奇怪的世界有任何关联吗？
t_en: Master, do I look like I have any ties to this bizarre world of yours?

t_ch: 看起来，和你的从者一样，他也来自另一个世界。
t_en: Looks like, just like your Servant, he's from another world.

t_ch: 我倒是松了一口气。幸好不是我所属的世界在未来变成了这副纸醉金迷的堕落样子。
t_en: That's actually a relief. At least this nightmare of indulgence and debauchery isn't my world's future.

t_ch: 他宝贝儿的腿，两个外地人召唤了两个外地人，这「圣杯战争」怎么也沾上了地域歧视？
t_en: Forkin' hell. So two outsiders summoned two more outsiders? The Holy Grail War allergic to local talent now or somethin'?

t_ch: 是三个。有三个来自其他世界的从者。
t_en: Make that three. There are three Servants from another world here.

t_ch: 哟！库丘林，被人家打回来了？
t_en: Oh! Cú Chulainn! Things didn't go your way, huh?

t_ch: 张嘴就把别人的真名报了出来…你到底有没有素质？
t_en: Did you just say my True Name out loud? Ever heard of tact?

t_ch: 有什么关系？这是个没人认得你我的世界，没人知道{RUBY_B#爱尔兰}艾林{RUBY_E#}，也没人听过「库兰的猛犬」的大名。
t_en: What's the big deal? No one here knows us. No one's ever heard of {RUBY_B#Ireland}Erin{RUBY_E#}, or the Hound of Culann.

t_ch: 不过这也意味着，我们不得不在劣势状态下，与受到本土力量强化的英灵们战斗。
t_en: But that also means we're at a huge disadvantage. We're fighting against Heroic Spirits who get buffed by this world's power.

t_ch: 哥们儿，就让你探查个情报而已，怎么能累成这样？
t_en: Man, all I asked you to do was a little recon. How'd you end up looking this beat?

t_ch: 哎，别提了，我原本能将两个御主一网打尽。结果，Assassin掩护御主一块跑了，另外一个灰毛召唤出了——
t_en: Don't even start. I almost got two Masters in one sweep, but then Assassin covered his Master's escape, and the other one... that gray-haired kid summoned—

t_ch: 喵！灰毛？！那是自己人啊，你爱{F#她}{M#他}干什么？
t_en: Fork! Gray hair!? That's one of ours! Why the heck were you fighting {M#him?}{F#her?}

t_ch: 你这家伙不是对我下达指令，要我逮着最欠揍的那些人收拾吗？
t_en: You're the one who told me to go after the most annoying targets!

t_ch: 唉，等和{F#她}{M#他}见面时，得好好澄清一番误会——嗯？你说{F#她}{M#他}的英灵也和你们一样？
t_en: Ugh... Guess we'll have to clear up that little misunderstanding when we meet — Huh? You said {M#his}{F#her} Heroic Spirit is like you guys?

t_ch: 没错，御主。那是一位……
t_en: That's right, Master. Their Servant is...

t_ch: …我俩绝不会陌生的Saber。
t_en: A Saber we're very familiar with.

t_ch: （惊讶）她？（冷静）还真是意外的重逢。
t_en: (surprised) Her? (calm) Well, that is an unexpected reunion.

t_ch: 她？还真是意外的重逢。
t_en: Her? Well, that is an unexpected reunion.

t_ch: 你们经常被召唤到不属于自己的世界吗？
t_en: Are you guys often summoned into worlds that aren't your own?

t_ch: 「不属于自己」？我们大部分时候都处在「不属于自己」的境遇里。时代、国家、战争，都是属于别人的。
t_en: "Aren't our own"? Most of the time, we're in situations that are foreign to us: someone else's era, nation, war.

t_ch: 这样面目全非的世界，我还是第一次见到。
t_en: But I've never seen a world so completely alien before.

t_ch: 不过，身为「抑止力的守护者」——你们不必细究那是什么——我原本就应当出现在「错误的圣杯战争」之中，也许那两位也是因为相同的原因，来到了你们的世界。
t_en: That said, as a Counter Guardian — you don't need to know what that means — my job is to show up when a Holy Grail War goes wrong. Maybe the other two were pulled here for the same reason.

t_ch: 明白了，我们是被你连累的。
t_en: Ah, I see. So it's your fault we're all stuck here.

t_ch: 「错误的圣杯战争」是什么意思？意思是说在你们的世界中，「圣杯战争」是个很常见的仪式，而在我们的世界中，老奥帝把这个仪式搞错了？
t_en: What do you mean by a "wrong" Holy Grail War? Do you mean that, in your world, the Holy Grail War is a common ritual, but in our world, Old Oti completely botched the process?

t_ch: 在我们那里也不至于「很常见」。但你们肯定是搞错了，首先，「圣杯」不是玩具。尤其不是你们这些魔术外行该去乱碰的玩具。
t_en: It's not exactly normal in our world, either. But you guys definitely screwed it up. First off, the Grail is not a toy. And it surely isn't something amateur mages like you should be messing with.

t_ch: 这一次，我们的肉身甚至都不是经由魔力构成的。
t_en: And this time, our physical bodies weren't even constructed from magical energy.

t_ch: 按照我的猜想，也许「圣杯」调用了与「魔术」性质类似的存在，构成了一场「拟似圣杯战争」。
t_en: If I had to guess, the so-called "Grail" must have tapped into something similar to magecraft to create this pseudo Holy Grail War.

t_ch: 被篡改的仪式，被某人占有的圣杯，作为参赛者的主办方——Lancer，还需要我说下去吗？
t_en: A corrupted ritual... a Holy Grail in the hands of someone who is the host of this war. Lancer, do you want me to spell it out?

t_ch: 简直像把「幕后黑手」贴在了自己的脑门儿上。好消息，这次的御主不像是个会滥用令咒的坏东西呢……
t_en: Might as well slap "Mastermind" on their forehead. Well, at least my Master this time doesn't seem like the kind of idiot who'd abuse their Command Spells...

t_ch: 那么，事情很简单，我们一起去把主办这场圣杯战争的老可爱给逮出来。
t_en: That makes things simple. We just find the muddle-fudger behind this Holy Grail War and drag them out into the open.

t_ch: 但如果他就等着我们上门，再把我们一锅端呢？
t_en: But what if they're waiting for us to come knocking, so they can wipe us all out in one go?

t_ch: …受不了，虽然很烦人，但也没办法。我应该是这里唯一受过正统魔术知识修炼的人。由我先试着去解析对方的布置，方便弄清他的目的。
t_en: Ugh, it's annoying but unavoidable. I believe I'm the only one here with formal training in magecraft. I'll try and analyze our enemy's setup, see if I can figure out their real goal.

t_ch: 至于你们，躲着也好，主动出击也罢，应该没有谁能威胁到你们的安全。
t_en: As for you guys... whether you hide or attack head-on, you should be safe for now.

t_ch: 一阵疾风拂过，蓝发的年轻人已消失在众人眼前。
t_en: A breeze blows past and the blue-haired man disappears.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}这时候给我发短信干什么？他宝贝的，这台词，{F#她}{M#他}给纯美骑士夺舍了？算了，反正也不是第一次见{F#她}{M#他}这样。
t_en: {NICKNAME}'s texting me now? What the fork? These lines... Did {F#s}he just get possessed by the Knight of Beauty? Whatever. Not the first time {F#s}he's acted like this.

t_ch: 这么快找你兴师问罪来了？
t_en: What, already hitting you up for revenge?

t_ch: 不，{F#她}{M#他}让我们去克劳克影视乐园，谈谈结盟的事情。
t_en: No. {M#He}{F#She} wants us to meet at Clock Studios Theme Park. Says {F#s}he wants to talk about an alliance.

t_ch: 与此同时，流梦礁
t_en: In the meantime, at Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 我本以为「英灵」的模样会…显得更特别？
t_en: I thought a Heroic Spirit would look... I don't know, more special?

t_ch: 这似乎也并非我的本来面目。但很抱歉，我只能以如今这样平庸的形象与您见面。
t_en: This isn't quite my true form. But I'm afraid this is the only way I can appear before you.

t_ch: 蒙您的召唤而来，我是…Caster。
t_en: Summoned at your request, I am... Caster.

t_ch: …我自然希望能与您坦诚相待。不过，每次我尝试说出自己的「真名」时，就会变成这样——███、███、███。
t_en: I would, of course, prefer to be honest with you. But every time I try to say my own True Name, it turns into this — ███, ███, ███.

t_ch: 「不协和音」…可是，那是你自己的名字。
t_en: Dissonance... But that's your name.

t_ch: 它被注册成了商标，而版权早已不在我的手中，抱歉，我的御主。
t_en: It was. But it's been trademarked. Copyrighted. Owned by someone else. I'm sorry, Master.

t_ch: 还是请叫我「知更鸟」吧。我不太习惯这样的称呼。
t_en: Just call me Robin. I'm not used to being called anything else.

t_ch: 知更鸟小姐，很遗憾让您失望了。我只是活跃在逐梦时代的一位过时的艺人，在娱乐业高速的新陈代谢中枯萎凋零的一块死皮。
t_en: Miss Robin, I'm sorry if this isn't what you were hoping for. I'm just a faded star from the Age of Dreaming, a piece of dead skin in an industry that sheds its past without a second thought.

t_ch: 我创造过辉煌。我是谁？也许翻翻那些故纸堆里早已被遗忘的旧杂志会有答案。我记得他们曾称呼我「永恒的银幕美人」、「希佩遗落的金色音符」……
t_en: I was brilliant, once. Who was I? Well... maybe you'd find the answer buried in some old, forgotten magazine. They used to call me the Eternal Silver-Screen Beauty, the Golden Note of Xipe's Legacy...

t_ch: 我曾有过更多的名字。但自出道以来就再也没拥有过自己的名字。
t_en: I've had even more names. But ever since I first stepped into the limelight, I've never had my own.

t_ch: 直到死去，直到又像如今这样被从坟墓中挖出，在这崭新的世界中重新登上我那陈旧的舞台，我仍只能对自己的名字避而不谈，稍有涉及就会被不协和音所取代——
t_en: Not even in death. Not even now, when I've been dragged from the grave to perform on this new world's old stage. Every time I even try to speak my name, it gets drowned out by that cursed dissonance—

t_ch: 但那是我的名字！我的名字！我的名字！
t_en: But it's my name! My name! Mine!

t_ch: 对不起……
t_en: I'm sorry...

t_ch: 没关系的，发泄出来吧，我完全理解。有时候我也想要情绪失控一下，不过在舞台上、在聚光灯下，身为表演者的我们只能强令自己平复。
t_en: It's okay. Let it out. I get it. Sometimes I want to lose control, too. When we're on stage, under the spotlight, performers like us have to keep our composure.

t_ch: 既然你没办法使用自己的名字…那我该怎么称呼你呢？
t_en: Since you can't use your real name... what should I call you?

t_ch: 按照「圣杯」赋予我的职阶，叫我Caster吧。
t_en: Caster. That's the title the Grail assigned me.

t_ch: 不，我不喜欢这样的称呼。我想要一个你选择的名字，而不是一个选择了你的名字。
t_en: No. I don't like that. I want a name you chose, not a name that was chosen for you.

t_ch: …谢谢你，知更鸟小姐。请暂时称呼我「音符小姐」吧。
t_en: Thank you, Miss Robin. Then call me Miss Note. For now.

t_ch: 我记得自己活着的时候并没有这么情绪充沛。大概是残缺的灵基对性格产生了影响。
t_en: Funny... I don't remember being this emotional when I was alive. Maybe my fractured Spirit Origin is affecting my personality.

t_ch: 没有「真名」，没有「宝具」，只剩这可笑的戏剧天赋。但我仍会竭尽自己微薄的力量，帮助你，也为我自己，赢取向圣杯许愿的机会。
t_en: No True Name. No Noble Phantasm. Just this ridiculous talent for theatrics. But I'll do what I can, however little that may be. I'll fight for you and myself to earn a chance to wish upon the Holy Grail.

t_ch: 你也想获得胜利么…但在我看来，唯一的「胜利」只有让战争消失。
t_en: You want to win? To me, the only real victory is making this war disappear.

t_ch: 而这恰恰是武器力有未逮，唯有歌声才能做到的事。
t_en: And that's something weapons can never achieve. Only song can do that.

t_ch: 可惜人们总会记得那些打赢战争的人，而不是那些力图让战争消失的人……
t_en: But people always remember the ones who win wars. Never the ones who try to end them...

t_ch: 无论御主想要令战争消失还是赢取胜利，都需要找到几个同盟。就像舞台上的演出不能仅有主角独舞一样。
t_en: Whether you want to end this war or win it, you're going to need allies. No performance is complete with only the lead on stage.

t_ch: 这也正是我在考虑的事情……如今加入圣杯战争的人选中，至少有两位是我信得过的朋友。我已经向其中一位发出了邀请，打算和{F#她}{M#他}开诚布公地聊一聊目前的情况。
t_en: I know. I was just thinking the same thing. Out of everyone in this war, there are at least two people I can trust. I've already invited one of them, and I plan to lay everything on the table for {M#him.}{F#her.}

t_ch: 匹诺康尼…城市像云朵一样在天空中飘浮着，冰淇淋和棉花糖会叽叽喳喳自动跳进你嘴里…实在不可思议。
t_en: Penacony... Cityscapes floating in the sky like clouds, where ice cream and cotton candy just chirp and jump straight into your mouth... This is incredible.

t_ch: 就像御主你说的那样，这是一个浸泡在梦中的世界。
t_en: Just like you said, Master. This world is steeped in dreams.

t_ch: 不过直到踏足这里，这个名叫「流梦礁」的地方，我才明白这确实是个会被「圣杯」选中的世界。
t_en: But it wasn't until we arrived at this place, Dreamflux Reef, that I truly understood why the Holy Grail chose this world.

t_ch: 为什么？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 就像每座金碧辉煌的宫殿都会有积满尘埃、不为人知的角落。在这些角落中，不被重视的人们依旧有着无数未被满足的愿望。这正是「圣杯」会降临此处的原因。
t_en: Because every shining palace has its forgotten corners, gathering dust, ignored. And in those places, there are people with countless, unfulfilled wishes. That's why the Grail would come here.

t_ch: 也正是这样一片土地，证明匹诺康尼并非梦国，它只是一处对现实的夸张投影。
t_en: This land proves Penacony isn't a dream kingdom, but an exaggerated reflection of reality.

t_ch: 先不聊这些了，快找地方吃饭吧，听着你肚子打鼓，我也饿了。
t_en: Alright, enough about that. Let's find something to eat. Listening to your stomach rumble is making me hungry too.

t_ch: 呃…身为从者现界时不会随身带着这个世界的钱。御主的钱包…没问题吧？
t_en: Uh... Servants don't bring money when they materialize in the world. Is your wallet... good to go?

t_ch: 流梦礁的快餐店量大实惠。
t_en: No worries. The fast food joints in Dreamflux Reef are cheap with big portions.

t_ch: 那我就放心了。作为一名久历战阵的从者，我向来不怎么讲究食物的品质。
t_en: Then I'm relieved. As a Servant who is a veteran of many battles, I don't really care about the quality of food.

t_ch: 放心吧，我有的是钱！
t_en: Don't sweat it, I've got plenty of cash!

t_ch: 那就好。我会用一场辉煌的胜利回报您的。
t_en: Good. I'll make sure to repay you with a glorious victory.

t_ch: 只要跑得快，就是免费的。
t_en: Well, if we run fast enough, it's free.

t_ch: 那可不行！骑士怎么能做这种吃白食的行为！
t_en: No way! A knight would never stoop to that!

t_ch: 如果实在没办法，我就只能在这儿做服务员来抵账了。
t_en: If it really comes to that, I'll just have to work as a server to pay it off.

t_ch: 快到了，Saber，前面就是「梦境快餐店」。
t_en: Almost there, Saber. Up ahead is the Dreamscape Fast Food Restaurant.

t_ch: 未知世界的餐馆…会吃到什么好吃的呢……
t_en: A restaurant in an unknown world... I wonder what kind of delicious surprises await...

t_ch: 亲爱的客人，欢迎光临，想吃点什么？
t_en: My dear guests, welcome! What would you like to eat?

t_ch: 哎？这不是咱们的盛会巨星吗？您这是来参加那个…「圣杯战争」的吧？
t_en: Wait a minute... isn't that our Festive Superstar? You're here for that... Holy Grail War, right?

t_ch: 流梦礁消息也这么灵通？
t_en: You guys know all the gossip around here too?

t_ch: 嘿，瞧您这话说的！我们流梦礁的消息最灵通了！
t_en: Oh, please! This is Dreamflux Reef, and news spreads like wildfire in this place.

t_ch: 我不是，我没有！
t_en: I'm not—! I'm not involved!

t_ch: 哈哈，不想暴露行踪对吧？放心吧，我懂，绝对不会出卖你的。
t_en: Haha, trying to stay low-key, huh? Don't worry, I won't sell you out.

t_ch: 旁边这位，恐怕就是您召唤的「英灵」了吧？斗胆问一句，英灵小姐，您是匹诺康尼历史上的哪位名人？
t_en: And this must be the Heroic Spirit you summoned, huh? Dare I ask, Miss, which famous person from Penaconian history do you represent?

t_ch: 抱歉，在「圣杯战争」中，「真名」是非常重要的情报，我不能告诉你。
t_en: Sorry, but in the Holy Grail War, revealing my True Name would be a major breach of security.

t_ch: 好的，我明白、我明白，就像配方要好好保密。
t_en: Gotcha, gotcha. Like a secret recipe, huh?

t_ch: 但能不能请你告诉我，这家店最招牌的菜品是什么？
t_en: Why don't you tell me this instead? What's the best dish on the menu?

t_ch: 如果您是第一次来流梦礁，那我强烈推荐您品尝一下咱们这里的「天环翅堡」——您在别处都吃不到这么好吃的汉堡。
t_en: If this is your first time in Dreamflux Reef, I highly recommend the Halovian Winged Burger. It's a burger you won't find anywhere else.

t_ch: 本店的面包以及其他的所有食物，所用的原料都来自纯净有机的忆域农田。它们生长的土壤不含有有害的情绪……
t_en: Our store only uses pure organic ingredients sourced from the Memory Zone's farms. The soil they're cultivated in is free of harmful emotions...

t_ch: 谢谢你，不必费心介绍了，就这个「天环翅堡」吧。
t_en: Thank you, but no need to explain. We'll go with the Halovian Winged Burger.

t_ch: 好，来<unbreak>100</unbreak>个！
t_en: Alright, we'd like <unbreak>100</unbreak> burgers!

t_ch: 好的…啊？<unbreak>100</unbreak>个？您确定吗？
t_en: Okay... wait, did you say a hundred? Are you sure about that?

t_ch: 确定。
t_en: I'm positive.

t_ch: 我身边这位小姐正是需要补充能量的时刻。
t_en: The lady next to me needs to replenish some energy.

t_ch: 让我想想…
t_en: Give me a minute...

t_ch: 那就五十个吧！我身边这位小姐正是需要补充能量的时刻。
t_en: Then make it fifty. The lady next to me needs to replenish some energy.

t_ch: 天环翅堡，不是天环能吃饱吗？
t_en: Just in case some of them fly away.

t_ch: 哇，你长得这么严肃可靠，怎么会讲冷笑话！
t_en: Wow, you looked so serious and reliable, and yet you're now telling bad jokes!

t_ch: 这么多天环翅堡，小店需要一些时间做准备。请两位客人稍安勿躁。
t_en: With so many Halovian Winged Burgers, it'll take a little time to prepare. Please be patient, folks!

t_ch: 说起来，Saber想对圣杯许下什么愿望呢？
t_en: So, Saber... what wish would you make to the Holy Grail?

t_ch: 如果有幸获得胜利，我希望……
t_en: If I'm lucky enough to win, I hope...

t_ch: 稍等一下再聊吧，御主。我感到有其他从者接近了。
t_en: Hold on, let's talk later, Master. I sense another Servant approaching.

t_ch: …两份天环翅堡，两瓶苏萨水。音符小姐，这样可以吗？
t_en: Two Halovian Winged Burgers, two bottles of Sousa Juice. Miss Note, is that okay?

t_ch: 当然可以…不过恐怕这里不是什么让人平静用餐的地方。我们面前，有个扎眼的从者。
t_en: Of course... but I'm afraid this isn't exactly a quiet spot to dine. We have a rather... noticeable Servant in front of us.

t_ch: 好久不见了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It's been a while, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 音符小姐，这位就是我刚才向你提过的「值得信任的朋友」。
t_en: Miss Note, this is the trustworthy friend I mentioned to you earlier.

t_ch: 原来…是御主的朋友吗？
t_en: Oh... they're a friend of yours, Master?

t_ch: 店主女士，麻烦把这两位的餐食也记在我的账上吧。此外，请您为我们保证一个安静的就餐环境，不要让其他客人打扰。我会另付小费的，谢谢。
t_en: Excuse me, could you please put their meals on my tab as well? Also, make sure we have a quiet dining experience, no interruptions from the other guests. I'll leave an extra tip for that. Thanks!

t_ch: 好嘞，您就放心吧，除了上菜，绝不会有任何人来打扰您的。
t_en: Alright, you can rest easy. No one's gonna bother you while you're eating.

t_ch: 那位从者小姐，收敛一下你的杀气吧。如果你的手时刻放在剑上…我也必须为保护知更鸟小姐做些什么了。
t_en: Miss Servant over there, could you tone down the murderous look? If your hand's always on your sword... I'll have to step in to protect Miss Robin.

t_ch: 这可是圣杯战争。请原谅，我会时刻处于迎击姿态，直到我能确信你们对我的御主没有恶意。
t_en: This is the Holy Grail War. Please forgive me, but I'll stay on guard until I'm sure you don't mean any harm to my Master.

t_ch: 放松一点，我们真的是朋友。
t_en: Relax, we're friends.

t_ch: 先试着好好相处一下吧！
t_en: Let's just try to get along first!

t_ch: 不好意思，打扰一下，几位客人，要打架也吃饱饭再打嘛！几位的天环翅堡来咯！
t_en: Sorry to interrupt, dear guests, but... if you're gonna fight, maybe you should eat first? Your Halovian Winged Burgers are ready!

t_ch: 那…先吃饭吧？
t_en: So... let's eat first?

t_ch: 同意。
t_en: Agreed.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…你们，点了这么多吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}... you two ordered all of this?

t_ch: 以矜持而优雅的姿态，两位从者将桌上的天环翅堡一扫而空。
t_en: With quiet poise and grace, the two Servants polished off the Halovian Winged Burgers on the table.

t_ch: 她们吃饱以后，似乎都冷静了下来。餐桌真是最适合外交的地方了。
t_en: It seems they've all calmed down after eating. The dinner table really is the best place for diplomacy.

t_ch: 很感谢你请我们吃天环翅堡。
t_en: Thank you so much for treating us to the Halovian Winged Burgers.

t_ch: 这没什么。我最喜欢看别人吃饱饭以后幸福的笑容了。不过，你的从者的食欲真是旺盛啊！
t_en: No problem at all. I love seeing people smile after they're full. But your Servant's appetite is truly impressive!

t_ch: Saber小姐，我不记得匹诺康尼的历史上有你这样的人物。
t_en: Miss Saber, I don't remember anyone like you in the history of Penacony.

t_ch: 因为我并非来自匹诺康尼的历史…我甚至，并非来自你们所在世界的历史。
t_en: That's because I don't come from Penaconian history... In fact, I don't come from the history of your world at all.

t_ch: 我…不太明白你的意思。
t_en: I... don't quite understand what you mean.

t_ch: 我是曾统治异界王国不列颠的骑士王，不存在于匹诺康尼的历史中，因此也不应该被这场圣杯战争召来。
t_en: I am the King of Knights who once ruled the kingdom of Britain in another world. I don't exist in Penaconian history, and thus, I shouldn't have been summoned to this Holy Grail War.

t_ch: 但实际情况却是，在这场「圣杯战争」中，除我之外至少还有一位来自异界的英灵。
t_en: But in reality, there is at least one other Heroic Spirit in this war who also comes from another world.

t_ch: 请原谅我问个最幼稚的问题：你们这些「英灵」究竟是什么？真的是历史名人死后的灵魂吗？
t_en: Forgive me for asking such a naive question, but... what exactly are you Heroic Spirits? Are you truly the souls of historical figures who have passed?

t_ch: 姑且这样理解吧——所谓英灵，是被历史所铭记之人。经由圣杯的召唤，英灵会以「魔力」构成的身体现界，并使用其生前知名逸话所形成的特殊能力…「宝具」。
t_en: Let's put it like this — Heroic Spirits are those who have been remembered by history. When summoned by the Holy Grail, they materialize in the form of bodies made of "magical energy" and use special abilities, "Noble Phantasms," which are based on their famous deeds in life.

t_ch: 呃，「魔力」、「宝具」…世上真的有这样的东西吗？
t_en: Uh, "magical energy," "Noble Phantasms"... Do these things really exist?

t_ch: 该怎么解释呢？这似乎是个神秘存在极为稀薄的世界，我所认知的「魔术」恐怕也无法如常生效。因此，这场「圣杯战争」中很多事物的存在方式都和过去不同了。
t_en: How should I explain this... This is a world where Mystics are extremely rare. The "magecraft" I know likely can't function as usual. So many things in this Holy Grail War are different from in the past.

t_ch: 构成我身体的不再是魔力，而是其他基本元素。而这位音符小姐，身为Caster的你，应该比我更清楚发生了什么。
t_en: My body is no longer composed of magical energy, but of other fundamental elements. But you, Miss Note, being a Caster, you should have a better idea than I do of what has happened.

t_ch: ……生前的我并非什么了不起的人物，能被当作英灵召唤，我也感到很惊讶。
t_en: In life, I wasn't anyone special. So I'm quite surprised to have been summoned as a Heroic Spirit.

t_ch: 我既没有Saber小姐那样强大的安全感，连自己的「真名」也丢失了。我实在不知道像我这样力量微薄的从者，能为御主做些什么。
t_en: I'm nothing like Miss Saber, who gives off such a sense of security and reassurance, I've even lost my True Name. I honestly don't know what a Servant as weak as I am can do for my Master.

t_ch: 音符小姐……
t_en: Miss Note...

t_ch: 即便如此，你作为英灵受到圣杯的召唤已是事实，这证明了你一定曾在历史上留下过独属自己的故事。而在圣杯战争中，英灵的力量也并非胜利的绝对条件。
t_en: Even so, the fact that you were summoned by the Holy Grail means that you must have left behind your own story in history. And in the Holy Grail War, the strength of a Heroic Spirit is not an absolute condition for victory.

t_ch: 说的不错。兄长曾对我说过：「匹诺康尼是一个空有传奇，却没有英雄的国度。」
t_en: Well said. My brother once told me that Penacony is a land of empty legends and no true heroes.

t_ch: 在他眼中，匹诺康尼的历史名人也好，传奇英雄也罢，都只是被后人包装而成半真半假的形象…是橱窗里供人消费的商品，连梦想也会成为真人秀的奖品。
t_en: To him, the historical figures of Penacony, the legendary heroes, are all just half-truths, images created by later generations, like products in a store window. Even their dreams turned into reality TV prizes.

t_ch: 但这一刻，无论是来自异界的Saber小姐，还是来自过去的音符小姐……我确定，你们是某种更真实的存在，绝不是受人包装而成的商品。
t_en: But at this moment, whether it's the otherworldly Miss Saber or the historical Miss Note... I'm certain you both are more real than that, and not just some commercialized product.

t_ch: 御主，感谢你的安慰。不过，以我如今真名遭到封印的状态，且不提与那位Saber小姐为敌，恐怕很难在其他对手面前保护你的周全，更别提帮助您实现梦想。
t_en: Master, thank you for your comforting words. But in my current state, with my True Name sealed, and not to mention being an enemy of that Saber, I fear it would be difficult to protect you in front of other opponents, let alone help you achieve your dreams.

t_ch: 我是橡木家系硕果仅存的主事者，同谐的调弦师，我并没有你想的这么脆弱。同样，事到如今的我，已没有想要托付圣杯的愿望了。
t_en: I'm the last remaining head of the Oak Family, a Chordmaster of Harmony. I'm not as fragile as you think. And as of now, I no longer have a wish to entrust to the Holy Grail.

t_ch: 御主…你到底在说什么啊？既然没有托付圣杯的愿望，又为何要接受令咒，踏入召唤阵，将我召来此处？
t_en: Master... what exactly are you saying? If you no longer have a wish to entrust to the Holy Grail, why did you accept the Command Spell, step into the summoning circle, and call me here?

t_ch: 对老奥帝先生所举行的这场游戏，我抱着半信半疑的态度加入其中…因为，一开始我心底确实怀有一个朦胧的想法。
t_en: I joined this game held by Old Oti with mixed feelings... because, at first, I did have a vague idea in mind.

t_ch: 我的兄长，他曾为了自己的一己之愿，召唤更高力量的介入，想将匹诺康尼打造成「沉眠中的理想国」。他失败了…也要为此踏上赎罪的道路。
t_en: My brother once summoned a higher power to intervene here for his own wishes, hoping to turn Penacony into a "utopia in dormancy." He failed... and now must walk the road of atonement.

t_ch: 但那场仪式所造成的影响，至今残留在匹诺康尼人的心灵角落中…如果可以，我想使用圣杯抹除、治愈那些创痕，弥补兄长犯下的过错。
t_en: But the impact of his actions still lingers in the hearts of the people of Penacony... I wanted to use the Holy Grail to erase and heal those scars, to make up for my brother's mistakes.
